Name: Shuckle
Effects: Draw 2 cards. Then, put a card from your hand on the bottom of your deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It stores Berries inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.

Name: Burmy
Flavor-Text: If its cloak is broken in battle, it quickly remakes the cloak with materials nearby.

Name: Wormadam
Effects: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon’s body. The cloak is never shed.

Name: Mothim
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Wormadam to another of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It flutters around at night and steals honey from the Combee hive.

Name: Snivy
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They photosynthesize by bathing their tails in sunlight. When they are not feeling well, their tails droop.

Name: Servine
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When it gets dirty, its leaves can’t be used in photosynthesis, so it always keeps itself clean.

Name: Serperior
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s attacks do 60 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
This Pokémon can’t use Slashing Strike during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It can stop its opponents’ movements with just a glare. It takes in solar energy and boosts it internally.

Name: Deerling
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: The turning of the seasons changes the color and scent of this Pokémon’s fur. People use it to mark the seasons.

Name: Moltres
Effects: You may do 40 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. It is said that its appearance indicates the coming of spring.

Name: Fennekin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: As it walks, it munches on a twig in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its ears.

Name: Fennekin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: As it walks, it munches on a twig in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its ears.

Name: Braixen
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When the twig is plucked from its tail, friction sets the twig alight. The flame is used to send signals to its allies.

Name: Delphox
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all Pokémon in play.
Flavor-Text: Using psychic power, it generates a fiery vortex of 5,400 degree Fahrenheit, incinerating foes swept into this whirl of flame.

Name: Delphox BREAK
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a [R] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
BREAK-Rule: Delphox BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Seel
Flavor-Text: Covered with light blue fur, its hide is thick and tough. It is active in bitter cold of -40 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Dewgong
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Its streamlined body has little drag in water. The colder the temperature, the friskier it gets.

Name: Omanyte
Flavor-Text: Revived from an ancient fossil, this Pokémon uses air stored in its shell to sink and rise in water.

Name: Omastar
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Restored Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Once wrapped around its prey, it never lets go. It eats the prey by tearing at it with sharp fangs.

Name: Omastar BREAK
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon-EX with his or her Active Pokémon.
BREAK-Rule: Omastar BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Glaceon-EX
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Evolution Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: White Kyurem
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [R] Energy attached to it, this attack does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

Name: Binacle
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: They stretch and then contract, yanking their rocks along with them in bold hops. They eat seaweed that washes up on the shoreline.

Name: Barbaracle
Ability-Effect: If you have a Stadium card in play, your opponent can’t attach any Special Energy cards from his or her hand to his or her Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Barbaracle’s legs and hands have minds of their own, and they will move independently. But they usually follow the head’s orders.

Name: Rotom
Effects: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Move 2 damage counters from each of your Pokémon to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

Name: Alakazam-EX
Ability-Effect: When you play M Alakazam-EX from your hand to evolve this Pokémon, before it evolves, you may put 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon and 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Alakazam-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Koffing
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Koffing and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Lighter-than-air gases in its body keep it aloft. The gases not only smell, they are also explosive.

Name: Weezing
Effects: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard 2 cards from the top of your opponent’s deck.
Both Active Pokémon are now Confused and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Very rarely, a sudden mutation can result in two small Koffing twins becoming conjoined as a Weezing.

Name: Mew
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can use the attacks of any of your Basic Pokémon in play. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Its DNA is said to contain the genetic codes of all Pokémon, so it can use all kinds of techniques.

Name: Spoink
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It bounces around on its tail to keep its heart pumping. It carries a pearl from Clamperl on its head.

Name: Grumpig
Effects: Put a Basic Pokémon from your opponent’s discard pile onto his or her Bench. Then, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses black pearls to amplify its psychic power. It does an odd dance to gain control over foes.

Name: Gothita
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: They intently observe both Trainers and Pokémon. Apparently, they are looking at something only Gothita can see.

Name: Solosis
Effects: If Solosis is on your Bench, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others.

Name: Duosion
Effects: If Solosis is on your Bench, this attack does 30 more damage. If Duosion is on your Bench, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When their two divided brains think the same thoughts, their psychic power is maximized.

Name: Reuniclus
Effects: If Solosis is on your Bench, this attack does 30 more damage. If Duosion is on your Bench, this attack does 60 more damage. If Reuniclus is on your Bench, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When Reuniclus shake hands, a network forms between their brains, increasing their psychic power.

Name: Diglett
Flavor-Text: Its skin is very thin. If it is exposed to light, its blood heats up, causing it to grow weak.

Name: Marowak
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks done to you or your hand by your opponent’s Pokémon. Remove any existing effects.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The bone it holds is its key weapon. It throws the bone skillfully like a boomerang to KO targets.

Name: Kabuto
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was regenerated from the fossil of an ancient creature. It protects itself with a hard shell.

Name: Kabutops
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A slim and fast swimmer. It slices its prey with its sharp sickles and drinks the body fluids.

Name: Larvitar
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Born deep underground, it comes aboveground and becomes a pupa once it has finished eating the surrounding soil.

Name: Larvitar
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Born deep underground, it comes aboveground and becomes a pupa once it has finished eating the surrounding soil.

Name: Pupitar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its shell is as hard as sheet rock, and it is also very strong. Its thrashing can topple a mountain.

Name: Regirock-EX
Ability-Effect: The attacks of your [F] Pokémon (excluding Regirock-EX) do 10 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Wormadam
Effects: If Mothim is on your Bench, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon’s body. The cloak is never shed.

Name: Riolu
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The aura that emanates from its body intensifies to alert others if it is afraid or sad.

Name: Riolu
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The aura that emanates from its body intensifies to alert others if it is afraid or sad.

Name: Lucario
Flavor-Text: By reading the auras of all things, it can tell how others are feeling from over half a mile away.

Name: Hawlucha
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: With its wings, it controls its position in the air. It likes to attack from above, a maneuver that is difficult to defend against.

Name: Carbink
Ability-Effect: Basic Energy attached to your Basic Pokémon can’t be discarded by effects of your opponent’s attacks, Abilities, or Trainer cards.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has slept underground for hundreds of millions of years since its birth. It’s occasionally found during the excavation of caves.

Name: Carbink
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent’s Pokémon-EX.
Flavor-Text: It has slept underground for hundreds of millions of years since its birth. It’s occasionally found during the excavation of caves.

Name: Carbink BREAK
Effects: Attach 2 Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your [F] Pokémon.
BREAK-Rule: Carbink BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Zygarde
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon is a [D] or [Y] Pokémon, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It’s hypothesized that it’s monitoring those who destroy the ecosystem from deep in the cave where it lives.

Name: Zygarde
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It’s hypothesized that it’s monitoring those who destroy the ecosystem from deep in the cave where it lives.

Name: Zygarde-EX
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 more damage.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Umbreon-EX
Effects: Discard as many cards as you like from your hand. Then, draw that many cards.
If your opponent’s Mega Evolution Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tyranitar
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may attach 1 [D] Energy from your discard pile to this Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck. This attack does 50 more damage for each Supporter card discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: If it rampages, it knocks down mountains and buries rivers. Maps must be redrawn afterward.

Name: Vullaby
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Their wings are too tiny to allow them to fly. They guard their posteriors with bones that were gathered by Mandibuzz.

Name: Mandibuzz
Effects: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon that has an Ability. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Watching from the sky, they swoop to strike weakened Pokémon on the ground. They decorate themselves with bones.

Name: Wormadam
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon’s body. The cloak is never shed.

Name: Bronzor
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Implements shaped like it were discovered in ancient tombs. It is unknown if they are related.

Name: Bronzong
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s attacks, including damage, done to your Benched Pokémon.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Ancient people believed that petitioning Bronzong for rain was the way to make crops grow.

Name: Bronzong BREAK
Effects: Discard as many [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon as you like. For each Energy card discarded in this way, choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon and do 30 damage to it. Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance. (You may choose the same Pokémon more than once.)
BREAK-Rule: Bronzong BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Lucario
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
If you have fewer Pokémon in play than your opponent, this attack does 60 more damage for each Pokémon fewer you have in play.
Flavor-Text: By reading the auras of all things, it can tell how others are feeling from over half a mile away.

Name: Genesect-EX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may return a Pokémon Tool card attached to this Pokémon to your hand.
Effects: Discard as many [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon as you like. This attack does 20 more damage for each [M] Energy you discarded.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Jigglypuff
Flavor-Text: If it inflates to sing a lullaby, it can perform longer and cause sure drowsiness in its audience.

Name: Wigglytuff
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The body is soft and rubbery. When angered, it will suck in air and inflate itself to an enormous size.

Name: Mr. Mime
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent’s hand.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is adept at conning people. It is said to be able to create walls out of thin air by miming.

Name: Snubbull
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Small Pokémon flee from its scary face. It is, however, considered by women to be cute.

Name: M Altaria-EX
Effects: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage and heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cottonee
Flavor-Text: Perhaps because they feel more at ease in a group, they stick to others they find. They end up looking like a cloud.

Name: Whimsicott
Effects: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Like the wind, it can slip through any gap, no matter how small. It leaves balls of white fluff behind.

Name: Diancie-EX
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, any damage done to your Pokémon by an opponent’s attack is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 50 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kingdra-EX
Effects: Discard any Stadium card in play.
This attack does 30 more damage for each basic [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Meowth
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Adores round objects. It wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

Name: Kangaskhan
Flavor-Text: If it is safe, the young gets out of the belly pouch to play. The adult keeps a close eye on the youngster.

Name: Aerodactyl
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This vicious Pokémon is said to have flown in ancient skies while shrieking high-pitched cries.

Name: Snorlax
Effects: This attack can be used even if this Pokémon is Asleep. If it is, this attack does 90 more damage.
Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its stomach can digest any kind of food, even if it happens to be moldy or rotten.

Name: Lugia
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, any damage done by attacks from your opponent’s Active Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

Name: Lugia BREAK
Effects: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
BREAK-Rule: Lugia BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Whismur
Effects: This attack does 10 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.
Flavor-Text: If it senses danger, it scares the foe by crying out with the volume of a jet-plane engine.

Name: Loudred
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The shock waves from its cries can tip over trucks. It stamps its feet to power up.

Name: Exploud
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
At the end of your opponent’s next turn, discard the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it.
Flavor-Text: Its roar in battle shakes the ground like a tremor–or like an earthquake has struck.

Name: Altaria-EX
Effects: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 60 more damage and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Audino-EX
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
This attack does 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Audino-EX
Effects: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Minccino
Effects: Discard a Pokémon Tool card attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon prefer a tidy habitat. They are always sweeping and dusting, using their tails as brooms.

Name: Minccino
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon prefer a tidy habitat. They are always sweeping and dusting, using their tails as brooms.

Name: Cinccino
Effects: Your opponent reveals his or her hand. Choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of your opponent’s deck.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Cinccino’s body is coated in a special oil that helps it deflect attacks, such as punches.

Name: Cinccino
Effects: Heal 90 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Cinccino’s body is coated in a special oil that helps it deflect attacks, such as punches.

Name: Alakazam Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Alakazam-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Altaria Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Altaria-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Audino Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Audino-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Bent Spoon
Description: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s attacks, except damage, done to the Pokémon this card is attached to. (Existing effects are not removed.)
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Chaos Tower
Description: Choose which way this card faces before you play it. This ↓ player’s Pokémon can’t be Confused or Poisoned. (If those Pokémon are already Confused or Poisoned, remove those Special Conditions.)Choose which way this card faces before you play it. This ↓ player’s Pokémon can’t be Asleep or Paralyzed. (If those Pokémon are already Asleep or Paralyzed, remove those Special Conditions.)
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Devolution Spray
Description: Devolve 1 of your evolved Pokémon and put the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your hand. (That Pokémon can’t evolve this turn.)
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Dome Fossil Kabuto
Description: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Kabuto you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Energy Pouch
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, put all basic Energy attached to that Pokémon into your hand.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Energy Reset
Description: Put as many Energy attached to your Pokémon as you like into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Fairy Drop
Description: Heal 50 damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has any [Y] Energy attached to it.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Fairy Garden
Description: Each Pokémon that has any [Y] Energy attached to it (both yours and your opponent’s) has no Retreat Cost.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Fossil Excavation Kit
Description: Put 2 in any combination of Helix Fossil Omanyte, Dome Fossil Kabuto, or Old Amber Aerodactyl cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Helix Fossil Omanyte
Description: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Omanyte you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Lass’s Special
Description: Draw a card for each of your opponent’s Benched Basic Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Mega Catcher
Description: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Mega Evolution Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: N
Description: Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, each player draws a card for each of his or her remaining Prize cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Old Amber Aerodactyl
Description: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Aerodactyl you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Pokémon Fan Club
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Power Memory
Effects: Discard 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Description: The Zygarde-EX this card is attached to can also use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Random Receiver
Description: Reveal cards from the top of your deck until you reveal a Supporter card. Put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Scorched Earth
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may discard a [R] or [F] Energy card from his or her hand. If that player does so, he or she draws 2 cards.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Shauna
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Team Rocket’s Handiwork
Description: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard 2 cards from the top of your opponent’s deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Ultra Ball
Description: Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can’t discard 2 cards, you can’t play this card.) Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Double Colorless Energy
Description: Double Colorless Energy provides [C][C] Energy.

Name: Strong Energy
Description: This card can only be attached to [F] Pokémon. This card provides [F] Energy only while this card is attached to a [F] Pokémon.The attacks of the [F] Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).(If this card is attached to anything other than a [F] Pokémon, discard this card.)

Name: Glaceon-EX
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Evolution Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Alakazam-EX
Ability-Effect: When you play M Alakazam-EX from your hand to evolve this Pokémon, before it evolves, you may put 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon and 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Alakazam-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Umbreon-EX
Effects: Discard as many cards as you like from your hand. Then, draw that many cards.
If your opponent’s Mega Evolution Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Genesect-EX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may return a Pokémon Tool card attached to this Pokémon to your hand.
Effects: Discard as many [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon as you like. This attack does 20 more damage for each [M] Energy you discarded.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Altaria-EX
Effects: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage and heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kingdra-EX
Effects: Discard any Stadium card in play.
This attack does 30 more damage for each basic [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Altaria-EX
Effects: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 60 more damage and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Team Rocket’s Handiwork
Description: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard 2 cards from the top of your opponent’s deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Alakazam-EX
Ability-Effect: When you play M Alakazam-EX from your hand to evolve this Pokémon, before it evolves, you may put 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon and 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zygarde-EX
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 more damage.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Regirock-EX
Ability-Effect: The attacks of your [F] Pokémon (excluding Regirock-EX) do 10 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: N
Description: Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, each player draws a card for each of his or her remaining Prize cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Shauna
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).
